privileged_info_summary: The night_ifr_enroute_setup task operates on real aviation data and software. KSFO (San Francisco International) and KDEN (Denver International) are actual airports used by pilots for cross-country IFR flights. IFR Low-Altitude Enroute charts are official FAA products used by instrument-rated pilots for navigation below FL180 (18,000 feet MSL). Avare is a real, open-source Electronic Flight Bag application for Android with extensive real-world pilot adoption (100,000+ installations, 4.8/5 rating). Night Mode is a standard feature in EFBs and cockpit systems to preserve night vision and reduce glare during night operations. The verifier correctly checks for Night Mode enablement via SharedPreferences, IFR Low chart selection, and flight plan creation with the two specified airports.
